All right, you're back and it's time to get very specific now. So in this first step, we did what? We got honest. We said, is there a problem? And why is that important? Actually, I want you to do the same way we do with belief exercise. I want you to really say, you know, what is a low standard cost me? I want you to give yourself reasons to change, make it very clear in your own mind. So it starts by identifying where you are. And the best metaphor is I use with the vision. I say it's like it's a car navigation system, right? Well, a car navigation system needs two things. It needs an accurate starting point and an accurate destination. The navigation system itself will get you there pretty much every time. Why? Because you have a chip in the car that's hooked up to all the satellites communicates. It shows the computer exactly where you are at any given moment. So if you just plug a destination in, the software shows you how to get there. Same thing is true of standards. So it starts by saying, OK, I have to travel. So the first step is like, do I need to travel somewhere? Do I got to go or my already my destination?
